title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Harrison, NY | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Harrison, Westchester. Safe, humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free quote!
intro: Bats in your Harrison attic or commercial property require immediate professional attention. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Harrison and Westchester County, ensuring safe, humane, and legally compliant solutions. Our certified technicians use proven methods to remove bats and seal entry points to prevent future infestations.
whyUs: With over a decade serving Harrison and surrounding Westchester communities, Best Pest Control NYC combines local expertise with state-of-the-art bat exclusion techniques. Our licensed team understands New York's bat protection laws and delivers guaranteed results with minimal disruption to your home or business.
localContext: Harrison's mix of historic homes near the Metro-North station and newer developments along the Hutchinson River Parkway corridor provide numerous entry points for bat colonies seeking shelter. The mature trees throughout neighborhoods like Sterling Ridge and Purchase create ideal bat habitats, making professional exclusion essential for protecting your property from guano accumulation and structural damage.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Harrison, NY?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Harrison typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the colony size, number of entry points, and the complexity of your property's architecture. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Harrison and Westchester County?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done properly. New York protects certain bat species, and removal is prohibited during maternity season (May 1 through August 15) when flightless pups are present. Our licensed team ensures all work complies with state and local wildlife regulations.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Harrison home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, dark stains around potential entry holes, and seeing bats emerge from your roof at sunset. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our process includes a thorough inspection to identify all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all secondary entry points, and a follow-up visit to ensure complete exclusion. We also provide attic cleanup and sanitation services to remove guano and restore your space.
